title: معالج القنوات
subtitle: إعداد قنوات المراسلة خطوة بخطوة
whatsappLoginDesc: امسح رمز QR باستخدام WhatsApp على هاتفك
weixinLoginDesc: Click the button below to generate a QR code, then scan with WeChat to connect your account
deleteConfirm: هل تريد إزالة هذه القناة وجميع متغيرات البيئة؟
sttEnabledDesc: تحويل الرسائل الصوتية الواردة تلقائياً إلى نص
streamingEnabledDesc: بث الردود إلى القنوات في الوقت الفعلي
telegramPrep: Open Telegram, search for @BotFather (or click the link above)
Send /newbot, follow prompts for name and username
Copy the generated Bot Token (format: digits:alphanumeric, e.g. 123456789:ABCdef-GHI)
For groups: /setprivacy → Disable (turn off privacy mode)
⚠️ After disabling privacy mode, you MUST remove and re-add the Bot to the group for it to take effect
For admin features (pin/delete messages): set Bot as group admin
telegramPitfall: تأكد من أن الرمز من @BotFather وليس رمز API
discordPrep: Open Discord Developer Portal → New Application (or click the link above)
Bot → Add Bot → Reset Token → Copy Bot Token (note: this is NOT the OAuth2 Client Secret)
⚠️ MUST enable Message Content Intent (otherwise Bot connects but reads empty message content)
Also enable Server Members Intent (if you need member lists)
OAuth2 URL Generator → Check bot + applications.commands
Select permissions: View Channels, Send Messages, Read Message History, Embed Links, Attach Files (use Permission Calculator)
Generate invite URL and add Bot to server
Note: Bots in 75+ servers require additional Privileged Intent approval
discordPitfall: فعّل غرض Message Content في بوابة المطورين → Bot → Privileged Gateway Intents
slackPrep: Create App at api.slack.com/apps (From scratch, or click the link above)
Socket Mode → Enable → Generate App-Level Token (xapp-..., requires connections:write scope)
OAuth & Permissions → Add Bot Token Scopes → Install to Workspace → Copy Bot Token (xoxb-...)
⚠️ Three different tokens: App Token (xapp-) for Socket Mode, Bot Token (xoxb-) for API calls, User Token (xoxp-) optional
Event Subscriptions → Subscribe to message.channels, message.groups, message.im, message.mpim, app_mention
App Home → Enable Messages Tab (otherwise users cannot DM the Bot)
Use /invite @botname to add Bot to target channels
After modifying Scopes, you must Reinstall to Workspace for changes to take effect
slackPitfall: استخدم رمز Bot Token (xoxb-...) وليس User Token
whatsappPrep: ⚠️ Gateway MUST run on Node.js (WhatsApp/Baileys is unstable on Bun, causes frequent disconnects)
Prepare a dedicated phone number (strongly recommended — unofficial protocol carries ban risk)
Ensure the number is registered on WhatsApp and can send/receive messages
Check linked devices count (max 4 — cannot connect if limit reached)
You will scan a QR code on your phone later (QR expires in ~20 seconds, be ready)
If connection fails, delete the session files in ~/.hermes/sessions/ and re-scan
whatsappPitfall: استخدم رقم هاتف منفصل أو eSIM موصى به
signalPrep: Install Java 21+ runtime (required by signal-cli — most common failure cause)
Install signal-cli (click the link below for latest version and install instructions)
ARM Linux users may need to manually compile libsignal-client native library
Prepare a dedicated phone number (recommended — Bot ignores its own messages to prevent loops)
Run signal-cli link, then scan QR code in phone Signal → Settings → Linked Devices
If signal-cli is not in system PATH, specify the full path in config
Note: Signal has strict rate limits — bulk sending can trigger throttling
mattermostPrep: Create a Bot Account in Mattermost admin console
Ensure Bot is added to target channels/teams
mattermostPitfall: يحتاج حساب بوت من وحدة تحكم النظام
matrixPrep: Register or deploy a Matrix Homeserver (e.g. matrix.org or self-hosted Synapse)
Create a Bot account and get Access Token (via /_matrix/client/r0/login API)
Homeserver URL must include protocol (e.g. https://matrix.org)
E2EE rooms may require additional configuration
matrixPitfall: استخدم رمز الوصول — كلمات المرور لا تعمل مع جميع الخوادم الرئيسية
dingtalkPrep: In DingTalk, tap "Create or Join Organization" → Create one (no documents needed, 5 minutes)
Sign in to DingTalk Open Platform open-dev.dingtalk.com → Switch to the new org → Create app
Add Bot capability in App Capabilities, message receive mode MUST be Stream (no public IP needed)
Get Client ID (AppKey) and Client Secret (AppSecret) from Credentials page
Create and publish a version in Version Management (bot appears in DingTalk search only after publishing)
(Optional) For AI Card streaming, enable Card.Instance.Write and Card.Streaming.Write permissions
dingtalkPitfall: فعّل صلاحيات Stream للبوت في وحدة تحكم DingTalk
feishuPrep: Sign in to Feishu Open Platform open.feishu.cn → Create enterprise app
Copy App Key and App Secret from Credentials page
Add Bot capability in App Capabilities, set bot name
Add required permissions: im:message, im:message.p2p_msg:readonly, im:message.group_at_msg:readonly, im:message:send_as_bot, im:resource (use "Copy Permission JSON" below for batch import)

In Events & Callbacks, select "Receive events via long connection" (recommended, no public IP needed), add events: im.message.receive_v1, im.message.message_read_v1, im.chat.member.bot.added_v1, im.chat.member.bot.deleted_v1
Create and publish a version in Version Management (required after every config change)
⚠️ Bot can only be found in Feishu search after publishing
feishuPitfall: تأكد من نشر التطبيق وتمكين أذونات الأحداث
wecomPrep: Visit work.weixin.qq.com to register and sign in to WeCom admin console
In App Management → Smart Bot, create a bot
Set callback URL: http://<your-ip-or-domain>:18789/wecom (ensure port 18789 is accessible)
Note the generated Token and EncodingAESKey (click "Random Generate")
⚠️ Configure HermesAgent and start the gateway FIRST, then go back to WeCom console and click "Create" — otherwise callback verification will fail
After creation, scan QR code to add the bot and start chatting
wecomPitfall: تأكد من تطابق عنوان الاستدعاء والرمز ومفتاح AES مع وحدة تحكم WeCom
bluebubblesPrep: Requires an always-on Mac running BlueBubbles Server
Download and install BlueBubbles Server (see link below)
Sign in with Apple ID and ensure iMessage works
Configure connection method and API password in BlueBubbles
Private API features require disabling SIP
bluebubblesPitfall: يحتاج خادم BlueBubbles على جهاز Mac
feishuPermJson: تطبيق Feishu يحتاج JSON أذونات التطبيق
wecom_callbackPrep: Log in to the WeCom admin console at work.weixin.qq.com
Go to App Management → Self-built → Create Application
Copy the AgentId and Secret (corpSecret) from the application detail page
Copy the Corp ID (corpId) from the My Company page
Under "Receive Messages" → "Set API Receive", fill in the callback URL (http://YOUR_IP:18789/wecom-app), Token, and EncodingAESKey
Add your server's public IP to "Enterprise Trusted IP"
⚠️ Start HermesAgent first, THEN click Save in the WeCom console — otherwise callback verification fails
wecom_callbackPitfall: ⚠️ Common pitfalls: 1) Must start the gateway before clicking Save in WeCom console — callback verification will fail otherwise; 2) Must add server IP to "Enterprise Trusted IP" or outbound messages fail; 3) Token and EncodingAESKey must match exactly between HermesAgent config and WeCom console; 4) Members can use the Bot through the WeCom Workspace entry in personal WeChat.
weixinPrep: Prepare a dedicated WeChat account (use a secondary number — automation carries a ban risk)
Ensure the account can send and receive messages normally in the official WeChat app
Leave the QR code ready — you will scan it with that WeChat account to link the session
After linking, sessions are persisted; do not link the same account to more than one HermesAgent instance
weixinPitfall: ⚠️ Common pitfalls: 1) Uses an unofficial protocol (iLink) — there is a non-zero ban risk, use a dedicated account; 2) QR codes expire quickly (~10 minutes); refresh if needed; 3) Do not run two HermesAgent instances linked to the same WeChat account; 4) On failure, clear the session file and re-scan.
smsPrep: Create a Twilio account at twilio.com and verify your phone number
Buy an SMS-capable phone number in the Twilio Console
Copy the Account SID and Auth Token from the Twilio Console dashboard
Configure a Messaging Webhook pointing to HermesAgent if you want inbound SMS
Alternatively, configure another SMS provider and map its credentials to the same env vars
smsPitfall: ⚠️ Common pitfalls: 1) SMS costs per-message fees — monitor usage; 2) Country-specific regulations may apply (A2P 10DLC in the US, etc.); 3) For inbound SMS, the Twilio number must have an SMS-enabled capability and a reachable webhook URL; 4) Rate limits apply — bulk sending may be throttled.
emailPrep: Identify your SMTP / IMAP provider (Gmail, Outlook, self-hosted, etc.)
For Gmail: enable 2FA and create an App Password (do NOT use your account password)
For inbound email, prefer IMAP IDLE (real-time) over polling; enable IMAP in the provider settings
Collect: email address, App Password, IMAP host+port (default 993), SMTP host+port (default 587)
Test credentials locally (e.g. with openssl s_client or Thunderbird) before pasting into HermesAgent
emailPitfall: ⚠️ Common pitfalls: 1) Gmail/Outlook refuse plain-password logins — you MUST create an App Password; 2) "Less secure apps" is deprecated on most providers; 3) IMAP IDLE connections need to be refreshed every ~29 minutes (handled automatically); 4) Self-hosted mail servers often need a correct SPF/DKIM/DMARC to avoid being rate-limited or silently dropped.
homeassistantPrep: Install Home Assistant and confirm you can reach its web UI (e.g. http://homeassistant.local:8123)
Create a Long-Lived Access Token: Profile → Security → Long-Lived Access Tokens → Create Token
Copy the token — it is shown ONLY once
Note the Home Assistant URL HermesAgent should reach (local IP or external HTTPS)
Optional: enable the Conversation and Assist integrations if you want voice-style commands
homeassistantPitfall: ⚠️ Common pitfalls: 1) Long-Lived Tokens are displayed once — store them immediately; 2) If HermesAgent is not on the same LAN, the URL must be HTTPS and resolvable; 3) Some integrations (e.g. locks) require additional per-device authorization; 4) Rate limits apply on public-exposed instances, consider Nabu Casa or a reverse proxy.
api_serverPrep: Decide whether clients will authenticate with an API key (recommended) or operate unauthenticated on localhost only
If enabling API-key auth, generate a random key (32+ bytes) and distribute it securely
Choose the listen host and port — default is 127.0.0.1:8765; use 0.0.0.0 only behind a reverse proxy
If exposing to the internet, front with a reverse proxy (nginx/Caddy/Cloudflare) terminating TLS
Review rate limiting and allow-lists before opening the port publicly
api_serverPitfall: ⚠️ Common pitfalls: 1) Do NOT bind to 0.0.0.0 without a reverse proxy + TLS + API key — the agent can execute tools; 2) API keys in query strings leak into logs; send them as `Authorization: Bearer …` headers; 3) Long-running requests may need higher timeouts on both sides; 4) Streaming responses require HTTP/1.1 keep-alive or HTTP/2 — old proxies may buffer and break streaming.
qqbotDesc: QQ Official Bot API v2 (guilds, groups, C2C)
